data.content:
**Diagnosis:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's implicit glob expands at evaluation time (before targets run), so the dynamically-created file is invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `Compile` and register it in `FileWrites` for clean support.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add generated file to compilation and register for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's implicit globbing happens at evaluation time (before any targets run), so the dynamically generated file isn't picked up.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group within the target, after the file is written. This is the classic "including generated files" pattern.

Let me fix this.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add generated file to compilation and register for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

    <!-- Add generated file to compilation and register for clean -->

    </ItemGroup>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. Here's the summary:

**Problem:** The target generates `GeneratedInfo.cs` at build time, but MSBuild's implicit file globbing runs at *evaluation* time (before any target executes). The generated file didn't exist yet during evaluation, so it was never included in compilation.

**Fix:** Added the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group *inside the target* (after writing the file), and registered it in `<FileWrites>` so `dotnet clean` removes it:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
  <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```
